stories.title: Xi pitches China as AI partner to developing world, warns against risks and security overreach
stories.simple_summary: China's leader is telling developing countries that China can be a good AI partner, while warning about risks from AI. This is mostly about China's international politics and business strategy.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect your daily life or small business unless you work in international trade — it's about which countries do business with which.

stories.title: Australia to Put Environmental Brakes on A.I. Data Centers
stories.simple_summary: Australia is putting limits on new AI data centers because they use a lot of electricity and water. AI companies need huge computer centers to run their systems.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live in Australia, this might keep electricity costs more stable; for everyone else, this mostly matters to tech investors.

stories.title: Alphabet shares fall on report its most powerful AI model Gemini 3.5 Pro is delayed
stories.simple_summary: Google's powerful new AI tool is delayed, and this news made Google's stock price drop. This is about investor concerns, not a product change you'd notice.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
stories.simple_summary: Google's video-making tool now lets you create videos with a fake version of yourself in them, without needing cameras or actors. You just type what you want and AI makes a video.
stories.why_it_matters: If you run a small business and need videos for marketing, this could save you money instead of hiring someone to film or act.

stories.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
stories.simple_summary: Roblox (a game-making platform for young people) now lets you create simple games by just typing what you want — no coding needed.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't directly affect most small businesses, unless you sell games or want to learn how AI helps non-experts create things.

stories.title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
stories.simple_summary: New York's governor is using AI to read through all the state's old rules and laws to find ones that are outdated or no longer make sense.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually affect you if state rules change, but it's mostly a government efficiency project — no immediate action needed.

stories.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
stories.simple_summary: Google's AI assistant can now do real tasks (like sending emails or managing your calendar) instead of just answering questions.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google tools for your business, this could save you time on routine tasks, though you'll need to set it up carefully.

stories.title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
stories.simple_summary: Google is renaming one of its AI note-taking apps from NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook — it's the same tool with a new name.
stories.why_it_matters: If you already use this app, you just need to know the new name; if you don't, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
stories.simple_summary: DoorDash is testing a way for AI programs to order food directly, like a person would. This is designed for software and robots, not regular people.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech companies building AI systems — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI released its first real product you can hold (besides software) — it's a basketball with ChatGPT built in. The details aren't clear from the headline.
stories.why_it_matters: This is probably more a fun novelty or marketing idea than something most people need.

stories.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
stories.simple_summary: A former AI researcher raised a huge amount of money ($300 million) for a new company about visual AI, before even releasing a product yet.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech industry people — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
stories.simple_summary: A startup CEO is disagreeing with other AI companies about using big words like 'superintelligence' to describe their AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly inside talk among AI researchers and investors about how honest companies should be about their claims.
